articles.title: Authorities allege man threatened officers with knife before fatal shooting in Westminster
articles.description:
"During that interaction, the suspect held a large knife and moved towards the officers," police said.

The post Authorities allege man threatened officers with knife before fatal shooting in Westminster appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Authorities report a man threatened officers with a knife in Westminster.
The incident led to a fatal shooting involving police officers on March 24.
Residents are concerned about police safety and community response protocols.

articles.title: Rachel Dratch has a hairdresser to thank for viral ‘Celebrity Jeopardy!’ moment
articles.description:
Lexington native and "Saturday Night Live" alum Rachel Dratch racked up millions of views on social media with her reaction to a clue on "Celebrity Jeopardy! All Stars."

The post Rachel Dratch has a hairdresser to thank for viral ‘Celebrity Jeopardy!’ moment appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Rachel Dratch's viral moment on 'Celebrity Jeopardy!' garnered millions of views on social media.
The Lexington native is a former 'Saturday Night Live' cast member, enhancing local pride.
Dratch's popularity boosts visibility for Boston's entertainment scene and local talent.

articles.title: She survived a brain aneurysm — now she’s finishing the Marathon Majors in Boston
articles.description:
After a ruptured brain aneurysm left her unable to walk, Andrea Calleja fought her way back — and will complete her World Marathon Majors journey in Boston.

The post She survived a brain aneurysm — now she’s finishing the Marathon Majors in Boston appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Andrea Calleja, a brain aneurysm survivor, will complete the Marathon Majors in Boston.
She previously struggled to walk after a ruptured brain aneurysm.
Her story inspires Boston residents facing health challenges to persevere.

articles.title: Young graduates face the grimmest job market in years
articles.description:
Artificial intelligence could reshape work, but for now a low-hire, low-fire labor market is the main impediment for young people seeking employment.

The post Young graduates face the grimmest job market in years appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Young graduates in Boston face a challenging job market with low hiring rates.
AI advancements are reshaping industries, but hiring remains stagnant for recent graduates.
The tough job landscape could lead to increased unemployment among Boston's young workforce.

articles.title: Pop-up bar dedicated to women’s sports now open in Boston
articles.description:
261 at Para Maria is named for the bib number worn by the first woman to officially run the Boston Marathon.

The post Pop-up bar dedicated to women’s sports now open in Boston appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: 261 at Para Maria pop-up bar celebrates women's sports, now open in Boston.
Bar named after the bib number of the first female Boston Marathon runner.
Local residents can enjoy a unique space honoring women's athletic achievements.

articles.title: Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to pay more than $224K over fraud charges
articles.description:
Cannon-Grant, who led the now-defunct nonprofit Violence in Boston, pleaded guilty to federal fraud charges last fall.

The post Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to pay more than $224K over fraud charges appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Monica Cannon-Grant ordered to pay $224K for federal fraud charges.
Cannon-Grant previously led the nonprofit Violence in Boston before its closure.
This ruling impacts local trust in nonprofit organizations and funding accountability.
